---
title: "Nish Etige"
# subtitle: "Nishchitha Sandeepana Silva Etige (he/him)"
toc: false
page-layout: full
image: assets/images/headshot2.jpg
about: 
  template: trestles
  image-shape: rounded
  image-width: 15em
  links:
    - icon: linkedin
      text: LinkedIn
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/nishchitha-etige-22713425/
    - icon: google
      text: Scholar
      href: https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=0IW8468AAAAJ&hl=en
    - icon: github
      text: GitHub
      href: https://github.com/nishsilva
    - icon: mail
      text: Email
      href: mailto:nishetige@gmail.com
---

::: {.home-hero}
# Hello there, Welcome!
:::

I am a Data Scientist specialized in developing data pipelines, analyzing complex and large datasets, and building statistical and machine learning models.

Over the past 8 years in academia and in the industry I have utilized numerical climate models, reanalysis, and remote sensing products to quantify the impacts of extreme weather and climate variability on physical and human systems.

Currently, I am building geospatial data exploration tools powered by AI/LLMs.

---

### Recent Highlights

::: {.callout-tip icon=false}
**Open to Work** — Actively seeking roles in climate data science, AI applications, and geospatial solutions.
:::

::: {.highlight-cards}
::: {.highlight-card}
**AI Tool**

[AI-powered Gulf Stream WCR Explorer](https://wcr-ai.streamlit.app/) — Interactive ocean feature detection tool.
:::
::: {.highlight-card}
**Publication**

[Machine Learning Review Paper](https://doi.org/10.1007/s44295-025-00076-1) — A survey of ML in ocean-climate science.
:::
::: {.highlight-card}
**Research**

[Science Advances](https://doi.org/10.1126/sciadv.adw3661) — Low-frequency sea-level variability modes.
:::
:::

::: {#edu-to-sidebar}
### Education

::: {.edu-timeline}
::: {.edu-item}
**PhD in Earth & Environment** · 2024

Boston University Graduate School of Arts & Sciences
:::
::: {.edu-item}
**MS in Marine Science & Technology** · 2019

University of Massachusetts Dartmouth's School for Marine Science & Technology | New Bedford, MA
:::
::: {.edu-item}
**BS in Aquatic Resources Technology** · 2015

Uva Wellassa University | Sri Lanka
:::
:::
:::

<script>
document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', function () {
  var sidebar = document.getElementById('about-sidebar');
  var block = document.getElementById('edu-to-sidebar');
  if (sidebar && block) {
    sidebar.appendChild(block);
    block.style.display = 'block';
  }
});
</script>
